questions.question:
Given two three-bit numbers a2a1a0 and b2b1b0 and the carry in c0, the function that represents the carry generate function when these two numbers are added is:

A. a2*b2 + a1*b1 + a0*b0
B. a2*b2 + a1*b1*(a2+b2) + a0*b0*(a1+b1)*(a2+b2)
C. a2*b2 + a1*b1*(a2 XOR b2) + a0*b0*(a1 XOR b1)*(a2 XOR b2)
D. a0*b0 + (a0 XOR b0)*a1*b1 + (a0 XOR b0)*(a1 XOR b1)*a2*b2
questions.answer: D. a0*b0 + (a0 XOR b0)*a1*b1 + (a0 XOR b0)*(a1 XOR b1)*a2*b2
questions.to_find: The carry generate function for a 3-bit adder with inputs a2a1a0 and b2b1b0

understand.plain: In a ripple carry or carry look-ahead adder, the carry generate function G tells us when a carry is definitely generated (independent of carry-in). For a multi-bit adder, we need to track when carry is generated at each bit and when it propagates through higher bits. This question asks for the overall carry-out generate expression for a 3-bit addition.

keywords.explain: A carry is generated at bit i when both inputs at that position are 1, i.e., G_i = A_i AND B_i. It means this bit position produces a carry regardless of carry-in.
keywords.example: For bit 0: G_0 = a0 * b0. If a0=1 and b0=1, carry is generated.

keywords.explain: A carry is propagated at bit i when exactly one input is 1 (XOR condition), i.e., P_i = A_i XOR B_i. It means if there is an incoming carry, it will be passed to the next stage.
keywords.example: For bit 0: P_0 = a0 XOR b0. If a0=1,b0=0 and carry-in=1, then carry-out=1.

keywords.explain: For a multi-bit group, the generate function aggregates: carry is generated if any bit generates it AND all preceding bits propagate it up to that point.
keywords.example: For 2 bits: G = G_1 + P_1*G_0 means carry generated at bit 1, or carry generated at bit 0 and propagated through bit 1.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='480' height='200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='140' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='80' y='40' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Bit 0 (LSB)</text><rect x='170' y='10' width='140' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='240' y='40' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Bit 1 (MID)</text><rect x='330' y='10' width='140' height='50' rx='6' class='bg'/><text x='400' y='40' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Bit 2 (MSB)</text><text x='80' y='80' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>G0=a0*b0</text><text x='80' y='98' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>P0=a0 XOR b0</text><text x='240' y='80' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>G1=a1*b1</text><text x='240' y='98' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>P1=a1 XOR b1</text><text x='400' y='80' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>G2=a2*b2</text><text x='400' y='98' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>P2=a2 XOR b2</text><text x='240' y='140' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>C_out = G0 + P0*G1 + P0*P1*G2 + P0*P1*P2*C_in</text><text x='240' y='170' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Group Generate = G0 + P0*G1 + P0*P1*G2</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Three bit positions (bit 0 = LSB, bit 1, bit 2 = MSB) each with their own generate (G_i = a_i*b_i) and propagate (P_i = a_i XOR b_i) signals. The group carry generate combines these: carry is generated at bit 0, or generated at bit 1 and propagated through bit 0, or generated at bit 2 and propagated through bits 0 and 1.

given.aim: Find the group carry generate function for adding two 3-bit numbers a2a1a0 + b2b1b0

terms.meaning: Carry generate at bit position i: carry is produced when both inputs are 1
terms.connects: Building block of the group generate function

terms.meaning: Carry propagate at bit position i: carry passes through when exactly one input is 1
terms.connects: Determines whether a carry from a lower bit propagates to higher positions

terms.meaning: Overall carry generate for the 3-bit group — carry is produced by the group independent of carry-in
terms.connects: This is exactly what the question asks for

given.plan: Expand the group carry generate formula G_group = G_0 + P_0*G_1 + P_0*P_1*G_2 by substituting G_i = a_i*b_i and P_i = a_i XOR b_i. Then match with the given options.

steps.title: Define per-bit generate and propagate
steps.formula_raw: G_i = a_i * b_i,  P_i = a_i XOR b_i
steps.apply:
G_0 = a0*b0,  P_0 = a0 XOR b0
G_1 = a1*b1,  P_1 = a1 XOR b1
G_2 = a2*b2,  P_2 = a2 XOR b2
steps.note: These are the fundamental carry look-ahead building blocks.

steps.title: Write group carry generate for 3 bits
steps.apply:
Substituting:
G_group = a0*b0 + (a0 XOR b0)*a1*b1 + (a0 XOR b0)*(a1 XOR b1)*a2*b2
This matches option D.
steps.note: The group generate is built from LSB upward: carry generated at bit 0, OR generated at bit 1 with propagation through bit 0, OR generated at bit 2 with propagation through bits 0 and 1.

steps.title: Verify against options
steps.apply:
Option A: simple AND of pairs — incorrect (misses propagation chain).
Option B: uses OR instead of XOR for propagate — incorrect.
Option C: uses XOR for propagate starting from MSB — wrong ordering.
Option D: a0*b0 + (a0 XOR b0)*a1*b1 + (a0 XOR b0)*(a1 XOR b1)*a2*b2 — this is exactly G_0 + P_0*G_1 + P_0*P_1*G_2, which is correct.
steps.note: Always expand from LSB (bit 0) upward for standard carry look-ahead convention.

solution.result: D. a0*b0 + (a0 XOR b0)*a1*b1 + (a0 XOR b0)*(a1 XOR b1)*a2*b2

questions.formula_note: Carry Look-Ahead group generate: G_group = G_0 + P_0*G_1 + P_0*P_1*G_2, where G_i = A_i*B_i and P_i = A_i XOR B_i.

questions.question:
In a look-ahead carry generator, the carry generate function G_i and the carry propagate function P_i for inputs A_i and B_i are given by:
  P_i = A_i XOR B_i  and  G_i = A_i * B_i

The expressions for the sum bit S_i and the carry bit C_{i+1} of the look-ahead carry adder are given by:
  S_i = P_i XOR C_i  and  C_{i+1} = G_i + P_i * C_i,  where C_0 is the input carry.

Consider a two-level logic implementation of the look-ahead carry generator. Assume that all P_i and G_i are available for computation. The number of AND gates and OR gates (each having any number of inputs) needed to implement a 4-bit look-ahead carry generator are respectively:

A. 6, 3
B. 10, 4
C. 6, 4
D. 10, 5
questions.to_find: Number of AND gates and OR gates needed for a 4-bit look-ahead carry generator (two-level logic)

understand.plain: A look-ahead carry generator pre-computes all carry bits simultaneously rather than waiting for ripple propagation. For 4 bits we need to compute C_1, C_2, C_3, C_4 using the given recurrence. The question asks how many AND gates and OR gates are needed for a two-level implementation of all four carry expressions.

keywords.explain: All carry expressions are implemented as Sum of Products (SOP) form: one level of AND gates feeding into one level of OR gates. No logic sharing between carry bits (separate gate trees for each).
keywords.example: C_2 = G_1 + P_1*G_0 + P_1*P_0*C_0 needs 2 AND gates (for P_1*G_0 and P_1*P_0*C_0) and 1 OR gate (3 inputs).

keywords.explain: Each carry C_i is fully expanded in terms of G and P signals and the initial carry C_0. This avoids rippling by computing each carry directly.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='500' height='250'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='10' y='10' width='480' height='220' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='250' y='38' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>4-bit Look-Ahead Carry Expressions</text><text x='20' y='65' class='sub ink'>C1 = G0 + P0*C0</text><text x='20' y='90' class='sub mut'>  AND gates: 1 (P0*C0)  |  OR gate: 1 (2-input)</text><text x='20' y='115' class='sub ink'>C2 = G1 + P1*G0 + P1*P0*C0</text><text x='20' y='140' class='sub mut'>  AND gates: 2  |  OR gate: 1 (3-input)</text><text x='20' y='165' class='sub ink'>C3 = G2 + P2*G1 + P2*P1*G0 + P2*P1*P0*C0</text><text x='20' y='190' class='sub mut'>  AND gates: 3  |  OR gate: 1 (4-input)</text><text x='20' y='215' class='sub ink'>C4 = G3 + P3*G2 + P3*P2*G1 + P3*P2*P1*G0 + P3*P2*P1*P0*C0</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Table showing the four carry expressions C1 through C4 for a 4-bit look-ahead carry generator, with the count of AND gates (each product term) and OR gates (one per carry) for each expression. C1 needs 1 AND + 1 OR, C2 needs 2 AND + 1 OR, C3 needs 3 AND + 1 OR, C4 needs 4 AND + 1 OR.

given.aim: Count the AND and OR gates needed to implement a 4-bit look-ahead carry generator in two-level (SOP) logic

terms.term: P_i = A_i XOR B_i, G_i = A_i * B_i
terms.meaning: Propagate and Generate signals assumed available as inputs to the carry generator
terms.example: P_0, G_0, P_1, G_1, P_2, G_2, P_3, G_3 and C_0 are all given
terms.connects: These are the primary inputs to all AND terms in the SOP expressions

terms.term: Carry recurrence: C_{i+1} = G_i + P_i * C_i
terms.meaning: Carry at each stage depends on generate and previous carry
terms.connects: Expanding this recursively gives the full look-ahead expressions for C_1..C_4

terms.meaning: Each product term needs one AND gate; all product terms for one carry output feed one OR gate
terms.example: C_2 = G_1 + (P_1*G_0) + (P_1*P_0*C_0): two AND gates + one OR gate
terms.connects: Counting AND and OR gates separately across all four carry outputs gives the answer

given.plan: Expand C_1, C_2, C_3, C_4 fully in SOP form. For each, count the AND gates (one per multi-literal product term) and OR gates (one per carry output). Sum up totals.

steps.title: Expand all four carry expressions
steps.note: Each carry has one more product term than the previous. Single-literal terms (like G_0 in C_1) do not need an AND gate.

steps.title: Count AND gates (multi-literal product terms only)
steps.formula_raw: AND gate needed for each product term with 2 or more literals
steps.apply:
C_1: terms are G_0 (1 literal, no AND needed) and P_0*C_0 (2 literals, 1 AND gate) => 1 AND gate
C_2: G_1 (no AND), P_1*G_0 (1 AND), P_1*P_0*C_0 (1 AND) => 2 AND gates
C_3: G_2 (no AND), P_2*G_1 (1 AND), P_2*P_1*G_0 (1 AND), P_2*P_1*P_0*C_0 (1 AND) => 3 AND gates
C_4: G_3 (no AND), P_3*G_2 (1 AND), P_3*P_2*G_1 (1 AND), P_3*P_2*P_1*G_0 (1 AND), P_3*P_2*P_1*P_0*C_0 (1 AND) => 4 AND gates
Total AND gates = 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = 10
steps.note: Single literals don't need AND gates — they connect directly to the OR gate.

steps.title: Count OR gates (one per carry output)
steps.formula_raw: One OR gate per carry output (C_1, C_2, C_3, C_4)
steps.apply:
Each of C_1, C_2, C_3, C_4 requires one OR gate to combine its product terms.
Total OR gates = 4
steps.note: Each OR gate has a different fan-in: C_1's OR has 2 inputs, C_2's has 3, C_3's has 4, C_4's has 5. But the count is still 4 OR gates.

steps.title: Final count
steps.apply: AND gates = 10, OR gates = 4 => Option B: 10, 4
steps.note: This matches option B. Two-level SOP means each carry is implemented independently with no gate sharing.

solution.result: B. 10, 4 — 10 AND gates and 4 OR gates are needed.

questions.formula_note: Look-ahead carry generation: C_{i+1} = G_i + P_i*C_i expanded to SOP form; AND gate count per carry output = (number of product terms - 1) since the single G_i term needs no AND gate. Total AND = 1+2+3+4 = 10, OR = 4.
